title: tragopogon borysthenicus
intro: Tragopogon borysthenicus, also known as the Ukrainian salsify, is a perennial plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant's roots have been used to treat various health conditions, including gastrointestinal disorders, and its extracts have been shown to have antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties, providing potential health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. In traditional medicine, Tragopogon borysthenicus is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it can also be consumed raw or cooked as a vegetable, showcasing its versatility in herbal preparations.
